2606.24089.title: DynaWM: Dynamics-Aware Distillation with World Model and Momentum Targets for Smooth Locomotion over Continuous Stairs
2606.24089.paper_summary: Recent advances in control have enabled bipedal-wheeled robots to traverse slopes and single-step obstacles, yet long staircase traversal remains challenging as current teacher-student frameworks suffer from weakened dynamics-aware representations and incomplete terrain geometry encoding. To bridge this gap, we propose DynaWM, a dynamics-aware representation learning framework. To enhance terrain encoding capability and enable transparent assessment, we introduce a world model as a regularizer to enforce forward-dynamics awareness, preserving comprehensive terrain geometry while facilitating hierarchical encoding visualization. To stabilize knowledge transfer, we employ a momentum target encoder to provide consistent distillation targets, preventing dimensional collapse from non-stationary teacher updates. Evaluation of the learned representations through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) visualization and quantitative metrics reveals that our encoder hierarchically captures terrain geometry with higher terrain encoding capability, leading to enhanced terrain adaptability and motion smoothness. Experimental results in simulation and real hardware demonstrate that our method achieves superior terrain adaptability and motion smoothness, enabling bipedal-wheeled robots to overcome diverse continuous stairs, as shown in Fig. 1.
2606.24089.paper_summary_zh: 近期的控制技術進展使得雙足輪式機器人能夠穿越坡道和單步障礙，但長樓梯的穿越仍然具有挑戰性，因為目前的教師-學生框架在動態感知表示和地形幾何編碼方面存在不足。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了DynaWM，一個動態感知表示學習框架。為了增強地形編碼能力並實現透明的評估，我們引入了一個世界模型作為正則化器，以強化前向動態感知，保留全面的地形幾何，同時促進層次編碼可視化。為了穩定知識轉移，我們採用了動量目標編碼器，以提供一致的蒸餾目標，防止因非穩定教師更新而導致的維度崩潰。通過主成分分析（PCA）可視化和定量指標對學習表示進行評估，顯示我們的編碼器以更高的地形編碼能力層次性地捕捉地形幾何，從而提高地形適應性和運動平滑性。在模擬和實際硬體中的實驗結果表明，我們的方法實現了卓越的地形適應性和運動平滑性，使雙足輪式機器人能夠克服各種連續樓梯，如圖1所示。
2606.24089.author: Haidong Hou et.al.
2606.24089.authors: Haidong Hou, Zhangguo Yu, Hengbo Qi, Jianlin Zhang
